content.problemStatement:
In the light of `G-20` summit, government has decided to keep the average air quality index (AQI) **strictly below** 100100100.

On some random day, Chef measures the AQI and found the value to be XXX.

Find whether the government was able to keep the AQI within limits.


Input Format

The input consists of an integer XXX — the AQI Chef measured.


Output Format
Output `YES`, if the government was able to keep the AQI within limits and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X — the measured AQI.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output "YES" if X is strictly less than 100, otherwise output "NO". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The only test case has X = 50, which is strictly less than 100, so the answer is YES.
X = 100 equals the limit; the requirement is strictly below 100, therefore the answer is NO.
X = 99 is less than 100, so the answer is YES.

solution.approach: For each test case read X and compare it with the threshold 100. If X < 100 print YES, else print NO. The algorithm is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
